product: TransitShield — NYC Subway Climate-Resilience Risk Index
note: All sources are public-domain U.S. open data requiring no API key or authentication.

sources.name: MTA Subway Hourly Ridership (Beginning 2025)
sources.role: Per-station annual ridership (2025 calendar year, subway mode), coordinates, borough. Drives the consequence term.
sources.access: Server-side aggregation: $select=station_complex,station_complex_id,borough,latitude,longitude,sum(ridership) as annual_ridership with $where transit_mode='subway' and full-year 2025 window, $group by station.

sources.name: MTA Subway Hourly Ridership (2020-2024)
sources.role: Alternate/historical ridership source (same schema). 2025 dataset used as the current-year basis; full-dataset group queries on this larger table time out, so 5wq4-mkjj was used.

sources.name: MTA Subway Major Incidents (Beginning 2015)
sources.role: Major-incident counts by division and line (2020-present used). Drives the station reliability index via line->station allocation.
sources.access: $select=division,line,sum(count) as incidents with $where month>='2020-01-01', $group by division,line.
sources.note: Task-listed id 'caer-yrtv' returned dataset.missing; correct id ereg-mcvp resolved via Socrata catalog search.

sources.name: MTA Mean Distance Between Failures (Beginning 2015)
sources.role: Division-level fleet MDBF (2025). Scales reliability index (lower MDBF => worse reliability).

sources.name: FEMA National Risk Index — Counties
sources.role: County (borough) climate exposure: coastal flood + hurricane + composite risk scores, Expected Annual Loss ($), and Social Vulnerability. Drives the exposure term.
sources.access: REST query where STCOFIPS IN (36005,36047,36061,36081,36085), outFields=*, f=json. Used because the FEMA static county CSV (hazards.fema.gov .../NRI_Table_Counties.csv) returns HTTP 403 (Akamai WAF Access Denied), NOT a proxy block. The ArcGIS FeatureServer serves the identical NRI values keylessly.
